domain: Star-nosed mole tactile fovea (Condylura cristata): 22 fleshy rays organized as 11 mirror-symmetric pairs; 25,000 Eimer's organs; 11th ray ventral pair is tactile fovea with 2x afferent-per-organ density; cortical magnification of fovea; foraging brings candidates from non-fovea to fovea.
specific_mechanism: Fixed anatomical fovea (11th ray pair) with disproportionate downstream representation; foveal innervation 2x density; content routing from periphery to fovea during foraging.
llm_application: STAR-MOLE radial-input foveal concentration: designate K=2 FIXED token positions p_f1, p_f2 (e.g., 0 and N/2) as foveal. Foveal positions get DOUBLE the attention heads (e.g., standard 32 heads, foveal 64 heads). Between layers, learnable content-router copies the highest-importance non-foveal token representation INTO a foveal position for the next layer. Output projection upweights foveal-position outputs by 2x. Differs from (a) Token Sparse Attention (uniform per position not fovea), (b) DeepSeek Sparse Attention (head-clustering), (c) attention sink (emergent not designed), (d) Lost-in-the-Middle (problem not solution), (e) RaaS (reasoning-aware not fovea-fixed) by combining (i) FIXED POSITIONS fovea + (ii) 2X HEAD count + (iii) CONTENT ROUTING into fovea.
novelty_claim: Attention-sink phenomenon emergent fovea-like behavior. Token Sparse Attention uniform. RaaS reasoning-aware. STAR-MOLE recombines (a) FIXED-POSITION fovea + (b) 2X HEAD allocation + (c) CONTENT ROUTING. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
